result: {"base_sha":"35d7d1307d7a2a455756b372d3bf637f2a5a382c","comments":["docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:211 — MEDIUM (reopen driver): §3's fault rule ('peer accepted but local membership cannot change → same-build session faults') contradicts the plan's own required success case 'need completion during a pending resize' (line 118) and §4's 'settled or faulted under the normal control loop' (line 244) never picks an outcome. The race is real at HEAD: workers retire naturally once the payload input closes and the shared queue drains (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:189-196), and a late accepted ADD already loses this race today — crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/data_plane.rs:1221-1225 finishes the socket cleanly when the pipeline is gone and crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:2063-2065 then settles it accepted=true at target, the exact false settlement ldt-1 outlaws. In the pull layout the DESTINATION initiator has already dialed the epoch-N socket before its ACK (mod.rs:3624-3639), so a literal fault also strands a live authenticated socket; §4.3's 'failed optional proposal settles at the unchanged count' (line 231) would instead desynchronize the peers after the destination decremented its logical count. Predicted observable failure: nondeterministic SessionFault on healthy, fully-transferred sessions whenever an ADD/REMOVE ACK crosses need-completion (both layouts), or a reimplementation of the forbidden false settlement. Fix (plan text, one prescription in §3/§4): an accepted op whose ACK finds the pipeline already draining/complete settles as accepted with membership satisfied by normal END retirement — ADD admits the member which immediately ENDs on the closed queue (today's clean-close made honest), REMOVE's decrement is satisfied by an already-END-retired worker — and the fault is reserved for a live pipeline that refuses or errs on an accepted operation.","docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:99-101 — LOW: the growth criterion accepts 'grow beyond eight workers' yet claims this 'proves that neither the retired target of 8 nor the old table maximum of 16 is a cap' (the 16 tier: crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs:691-693). A deterministic-trace guard passing at 9 streams satisfies the criterion while a latent 16 clamp (e.g. a renamed rather than deleted constant) survives unproved. Require the clean-trace guard to cross 16 (or run to the receiver ceiling) so the assertion matches the claim.","crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs:444-448 — observation, no change required: the blocked-ratio signal reads a source-/disk-bound sender (bytes moving, writes never blocking) identically to a clean pipe, so the retained initial policy will ratchet ADDs toward the receiver ceiling with idle sockets on source-bound transfers and never shrink them (idle workers accrue no blocked time, so the REMOVE signal cannot fire). Bounded by the receiver ceiling and correctness-neutral; the plan's explicit policy freeze (lines 56-59) plus the ldt-3 decision-reason observer make it visible and correctable through the loop — ldt-4 should expect and record this signature rather than grading it as a tuner defect.","Architecture questions verified clean: the static table is eliminated, not renamed or raced — its only two production call sites (crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/data_plane.rs:167 zero-knowledge grant, :1110 shape ramp) plus settle_shape_resizes and the accumulators are all on the deletion list, epoch-0 becomes a constant workload-independent floor, and no other TCP worker-count authority exists (hidden --workers is local-apply-only; dial max_streams/DIAL_FLOOR_MAX_STREAMS=8 have no production readers and are deleted/renamed). SOURCE ownership is structurally identical across layouts: both constructors build the same state with only SourceSockets::{Dial,Accept} differing, and the DESTINATION profile is available at grant-mint in both layouts (SessionOpen.receiver_capacity field 12 for the initiator-DESTINATION, SessionAccept field 1 plus the responder's own profile otherwise), so min(DIAL_FLOOR_INITIAL_STREAMS=4, receiver ceiling) — the dial's reviewed conservative tier — finalizes in SessionAccept without delaying first byte or exceeding the advertised bound. Tokenless REMOVE matches the existing proto (DATA_PLANE_RESIZE_OP_REMOVE, empty sub_token) and the proven pipeline substrate (watch-based RetireOne, exactly-once dequeue, END-driven receiver teardown, as the deleted ue-r2-2 push wiring ran live). The four slices avoid dual-policy windows (ldt-2 deletes shape policy in the same slice that activates live policy, with guards and contract in-slice); the local-apply and in-stream non-goals are real resource boundaries (LOCAL_SMALL_FILE_PATH.md owns filesystem workers, no socket to dial; the in-stream carrier has one lane), not hidden role disparities; acceptance criteria prove role invariance via identical epoch/op/target sequences and adaptive traces rather than a preselected worker count.","Independent guard proof: scripts/agent/check-docs.sh and git diff --check green on reviewed bytes; 33 semantic assertions over LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md, ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md, TRANSFER_SESSION.md, and STATE.md (SOURCE-only ownership, non-idle live telemetry, ADD and REMOVE, both initiator layouts, receiver safety ceiling, no static terminal target, exact-8 narrowed to static parity) GREEN on reviewed bytes; replacing only ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md and TRANSFER_SESSION.md with byte-verified base-SHA blobs (ac24af6/f7a3083) turned the guard RED with exactly the predicted shape-only contradiction — 17 failures, all on those two files, spanning both missing live-dial language and the forbidden 'one and only stream policy'/'shape-corrects upward' authority claims, while LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md and STATE.md assertions stayed green; non-interactive git restore --source=<reviewed SHA> returned hash-verified reviewed blobs (09bf955/406a4d4), the same assertions and docs gate ran GREEN, and the worktree ended clean at 554d080839e1419c2242921e444d40d02c947815. No endpoint, SSH, rig, push, branch, or deletion was touched; only the two named docs were mutated and exactly restored."],"guard_confirmed":true,"reviewed_sha":"554d080839e1419c2242921e444d40d02c947815","verdict":"reopened"}

comments: docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:211 — MEDIUM (reopen driver): §3's fault rule ('peer accepted but local membership cannot change → same-build session faults') contradicts the plan's own required success case 'need completion during a pending resize' (line 118) and §4's 'settled or faulted under the normal control loop' (line 244) never picks an outcome. The race is real at HEAD: workers retire naturally once the payload input closes and the shared queue drains (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:189-196), and a late accepted ADD already loses this race today — crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/data_plane.rs:1221-1225 finishes the socket cleanly when the pipeline is gone and crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:2063-2065 then settles it accepted=true at target, the exact false settlement ldt-1 outlaws. In the pull layout the DESTINATION initiator has already dialed the epoch-N socket before its ACK (mod.rs:3624-3639), so a literal fault also strands a live authenticated socket; §4.3's 'failed optional proposal settles at the unchanged count' (line 231) would instead desynchronize the peers after the destination decremented its logical count. Predicted observable failure: nondeterministic SessionFault on healthy, fully-transferred sessions whenever an ADD/REMOVE ACK crosses need-completion (both layouts), or a reimplementation of the forbidden false settlement. Fix (plan text, one prescription in §3/§4): an accepted op whose ACK finds the pipeline already draining/complete settles as accepted with membership satisfied by normal END retirement — ADD admits the member which immediately ENDs on the closed queue (today's clean-close made honest), REMOVE's decrement is satisfied by an already-END-retired worker — and the fault is reserved for a live pipeline that refuses or errs on an accepted operation.

docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:99-101 — LOW: the growth criterion accepts 'grow beyond eight workers' yet claims this 'proves that neither the retired target of 8 nor the old table maximum of 16 is a cap' (the 16 tier: crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs:691-693). A deterministic-trace guard passing at 9 streams satisfies the criterion while a latent 16 clamp (e.g. a renamed rather than deleted constant) survives unproved. Require the clean-trace guard to cross 16 (or run to the receiver ceiling) so the assertion matches the claim.

crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs:444-448 — observation, no change required: the blocked-ratio signal reads a source-/disk-bound sender (bytes moving, writes never blocking) identically to a clean pipe, so the retained initial policy will ratchet ADDs toward the receiver ceiling with idle sockets on source-bound transfers and never shrink them (idle workers accrue no blocked time, so the REMOVE signal cannot fire). Bounded by the receiver ceiling and correctness-neutral; the plan's explicit policy freeze (lines 56-59) plus the ldt-3 decision-reason observer make it visible and correctable through the loop — ldt-4 should expect and record this signature rather than grading it as a tuner defect.

Architecture questions verified clean: the static table is eliminated, not renamed or raced — its only two production call sites (crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/data_plane.rs:167 zero-knowledge grant, :1110 shape ramp) plus settle_shape_resizes and the accumulators are all on the deletion list, epoch-0 becomes a constant workload-independent floor, and no other TCP worker-count authority exists (hidden --workers is local-apply-only; dial max_streams/DIAL_FLOOR_MAX_STREAMS=8 have no production readers and are deleted/renamed). SOURCE ownership is structurally identical across layouts: both constructors build the same state with only SourceSockets::{Dial,Accept} differing, and the DESTINATION profile is available at grant-mint in both layouts (SessionOpen.receiver_capacity field 12 for the initiator-DESTINATION, SessionAccept field 1 plus the responder's own profile otherwise), so min(DIAL_FLOOR_INITIAL_STREAMS=4, receiver ceiling) — the dial's reviewed conservative tier — finalizes in SessionAccept without delaying first byte or exceeding the advertised bound. Tokenless REMOVE matches the existing proto (DATA_PLANE_RESIZE_OP_REMOVE, empty sub_token) and the proven pipeline substrate (watch-based RetireOne, exactly-once dequeue, END-driven receiver teardown, as the deleted ue-r2-2 push wiring ran live). The four slices avoid dual-policy windows (ldt-2 deletes shape policy in the same slice that activates live policy, with guards and contract in-slice); the local-apply and in-stream non-goals are real resource boundaries (LOCAL_SMALL_FILE_PATH.md owns filesystem workers, no socket to dial; the in-stream carrier has one lane), not hidden role disparities; acceptance criteria prove role invariance via identical epoch/op/target sequences and adaptive traces rather than a preselected worker count.

Independent guard proof: scripts/agent/check-docs.sh and git diff --check green on reviewed bytes; 33 semantic assertions over LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md, ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md, TRANSFER_SESSION.md, and STATE.md (SOURCE-only ownership, non-idle live telemetry, ADD and REMOVE, both initiator layouts, receiver safety ceiling, no static terminal target, exact-8 narrowed to static parity) GREEN on reviewed bytes; replacing only ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md and TRANSFER_SESSION.md with byte-verified base-SHA blobs (ac24af6/f7a3083) turned the guard RED with exactly the predicted shape-only contradiction — 17 failures, all on those two files, spanning both missing live-dial language and the forbidden 'one and only stream policy'/'shape-corrects upward' authority claims, while LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md and STATE.md assertions stayed green; non-interactive git restore --source=<reviewed SHA> returned hash-verified reviewed blobs (09bf955/406a4d4), the same assertions and docs gate ran GREEN, and the worktree ended clean at 554d080839e1419c2242921e444d40d02c947815. No endpoint, SSH, rig, push, branch, or deletion was touched; only the two named docs were mutated and exactly restored.
